html:
[626]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 本应绝嗣 - Literally 'should have been cut off,' referring to the expectation that the family line would end.
  • 贸贩平准 - Literal meaning 'trading and buying/selling at fair prices,' indicating a fair and just business practice.
  • 自公绝望 - Literal meaning 'from his own despair,' suggesting a sense of hopelessness or resignation.
  • 此真妄想 - Literal meaning 'this is truly a wild dream,' expressing disbelief or skepticism.
  • 两人皆将就木 - Literal meaning 'both of you are on the brink of death,' indicating extreme old age.
  • 忽梦神告曰 - Literal meaning 'suddenly dreamed that a deity told him,' describing a dream vision.
  • 此非安乐乡 - Literal meaning 'this is not a place of ease and comfort,' indicating that the place is not suitable for rest.
  • 茅庐可以下榻 - Literal meaning 'a thatched hut can serve as a place to rest,' offering a simple but welcoming accommodation.
  • 舅家丧葬 - Literal meaning 'the funeral of his maternal uncle's family,' referring to a specific event.
  • 暮归，路经华岳 - Literal meaning 'returning at dusk, passing by Mount Hua,' indicating the time and location of the event.
  • 迷窜山谷中 - Literal meaning 'lost and wandering in the mountain valleys,' describing a state of confusion and disorientation.
  • 心大恐 - Literal meaning 'greatly afraid,' expressing intense fear.
  • 一矢之外 - Literal meaning 'beyond the range of an arrow,' indicating a short distance.
  • 趋投之 - Literal meaning 'rushed towards it,' describing a hurried action.
  • 数武中 - Literal meaning 'within several steps,' indicating a short distance.
  • 欻见一叟 - Literal meaning 'suddenly saw an old man,' describing a sudden appearance.
  • 伛偻曳杖 - Literal meaning 'bent over, dragging a cane,' describing the old man's appearance.
  • 斜径疾行 - Literal meaning 'walking quickly along a winding path,' describing the old man's movement.
  • 方欲致问 - Literal meaning 'was about to ask,' indicating the intention to inquire.
  • 叟先诘谁何 - Literal meaning 'the old man first asked who he was,' describing the old man's initial question.
  • 以迷途告 - Literal meaning 'told him he was lost,' indicating the reason for the inquiry.
  • 且言灯火处必是山村 - Literal meaning 'and said that the place with lights must be a village,' expressing a belief or assumption.
  • 叟曰 - Literal meaning 'the old man said,' introducing the old man's response.
  • 幸老夫来，可从去 - Literal meaning 'fortunately, I am here, you can follow me,' offering help.
  • 大悦，从行里许 - Literal meaning 'greatly pleased, they followed for a short distance,' describing the response and action.
  • 睹小村 - Literal meaning 'saw a small village,' indicating the discovery of a village.
  • 叟扣荆扉 - Literal meaning 'the old man knocked on the bamboo door,' describing the action.
  • 一妪出，启关曰 - Literal meaning 'an old woman came out, opened the door and said,' introducing the old woman and her response.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 利津金永年 - Refers to Jin Yongnian of Lijin, a specific person and place.
  • 利津 - Place name, likely a county or town in China.
  • 媪 - Old woman, referring to the wife of Jin Yongnian.
  • 舅家丧葬 - Refers to the funeral of his maternal uncle's family, a specific event in the family.
  • 华岳 - Mount Hua, one of the Five Great Mountains in China, known for its steep cliffs and scenic beauty.
  • 茅庐 - Thatched hut, a simple and humble dwelling, often associated with rural or rustic settings.
  • 荆扉 - Bamboo door, a simple and rustic door, often used in rural settings.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '忽梦神告曰' introduces a dream vision, which can be ambiguous in terms of the dreamer's state of consciousness and the nature of the vision.
  • The phrase '此真妄想' can be interpreted as either a literal dream or a metaphorical expression of disbelief.
  • The phrase '两人皆将就木' can be ambiguous in terms of the exact meaning of '就木' (on the brink of death) and the emotional state of the characters.
  • The phrase '此非安乐乡' can be ambiguous in terms of the exact meaning of '安乐乡' (place of ease and comfort) and the old man's intention.
  • The phrase '幸老夫来，可从去' can be ambiguous in terms of the old man's exact role and the nature of his offer.

Proper Nouns

  • 利津 - Place name, likely a county or town in China.
  • 华岳 - Mount Hua, one of the Five Great Mountains in China.
  • 茅庐 - Thatched hut, a simple and humble dwelling.
  • 荆扉 - Bamboo door, a simple and rustic door.

[628]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 斟酌移时 - Indicates that time passed as they deliberated or considered something.
  • 频来行酒 - Means 'frequently serving wine,' suggesting the woman was actively engaged in pouring wine.
  • 嫣然含笑 - Describes a charming, shy smile, often used to depict a woman's grace and modesty.
  • 殊不羞涩 - Means 'not at all shy or embarrassed,' indicating a lack of modesty or hesitation.
  • 注目情动 - Means 'to gaze with emotion stirred,' suggesting a romantic or passionate feeling.
  • 睹仙容 - Means 'to see a celestial face,' often used to describe someone of extraordinary beauty.
  • 欲通媒妁 - Means 'to seek a matchmaker,' indicating an intention to arrange a marriage.
  • 恐其不遂 - Means 'fear that it might not succeed,' expressing concern about the outcome.
  • 默若不闻 - Means 'silent as if not hearing,' indicating a lack of response or reaction.
  • 屡问不对 - Means 'repeatedly asked but not answered,' indicating persistent questioning without a response.
  • 渐入室 - Means 'gradually entering the room,' suggesting a slow and cautious movement.
  • 厉色曰 - Means 'said sternly,' indicating a serious or angry tone.
  • 狂郎人闼 - Means 'rash man enters the room,' describing someone who enters without permission.
  • 将何为 - Means 'what is your intention,' asking about the purpose or reason.
  • 长跪哀之 - Means 'prostrate oneself in sorrow,' indicating a deep emotional plea.
  • 夺门欲去 - Means 'rush to the door to leave,' suggesting an attempt to escape.
  • 暴起要遮 - Means 'suddenly rise to block,' indicating a quick and forceful action.
  • 狎接臄 - Means 'to kiss intimately,' describing a passionate or intimate act.
  • 颤声疾呼 - Means 'shaking voice calling out,' indicating a frightened or urgent cry.
  • 匆遽入问 - Means 'hurriedly enter to ask,' suggesting a quick and concerned response.
  • 释手而出 - Means 'let go and leave,' indicating a release of physical contact and departure.
  • 从容向父 - Means 'calmly address the father,' indicating a composed and respectful manner.
  • 酒复涌沸 - Means 'the wine is boiling again,' suggesting the wine is overheating.
  • 非郎君来，壶子融化矣 - Means 'If not for the young man, the wine pot would have melted,' indicating that the wine was about to boil over.
  • 心始安妥 - Means 'the heart finally calms down,' indicating a sense of relief.
  • 益德之 - Means 'more and more admire her,' indicating growing appreciation or admiration.
  • 魂魄颠倒 - Means 'soul and spirit are confused,' indicating a state of emotional turmoil.
  • 丧所怀来 - Means 'lose what was intended to bring,' indicating a sense of disappointment or loss.
  • 伪醉离席 - Means 'pretend to be drunk and leave the table,' suggesting an excuse to depart.
  • 阖扉乃出 - Means 'close the door and then leave,' indicating a final act of departure.

[629]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 安不寐 - Restless and unable to sleep.
  • 未曙 - Before dawn.
  • 呼别 - Bid farewell.
  • 浼交好者造庐求聘 - Asked close friends to visit a certain person's home to arrange a marriage.
  • 竟莫得其居里 - Ultimately could not find the person's residence.
  • 强啖汤粥 - Forced himself to eat soup and porridge.
  • 唾欲吐 - Desire to vomit.
  • 溃乱中，辄呼花姑子 - In a state of confusion, would call out for '花姑子'.
  • 家人不解，但终夜环伺之 - Family did not understand his condition but watched over him all night.
  • 气势阽危 - In a critical state.
  • 守者困怠并寐 - Watcher became exhausted and fell asleep.
  • 矇瞳中 - State of drowsiness or half-consciousness.
  • 觉有人揣而抁之 - Felt someone touching and stroking him.
  • 略开眸 - Slightly opened his eyes.
  • 潸潸涕堕 - Shed tears.
  • 痴儿何至此耶 - Rhetorical question expressing surprise or concern.
  • 登榻，坐安股上 - Climbed onto the bed and sat on his thigh.
  • 以两手为按太阳穴 - Pressed his temples with both hands.
  • 穿鼻沁骨 - Strong, penetrating scent.
  • 汗满天庭，渐达肢体 - Sweat filled his forehead and gradually spread to his limbs.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 无 - Common character in Classical Chinese, often used to indicate negation or absence.
  • 花姑子 - Nickname or specific person's name, possibly a close friend or family member.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence of actions must be read as a continuous narrative: the narrator was restless, unable to sleep, and bid farewell before dawn.
  • This clause describes the narrator returning home, asking friends to visit a certain person's home to arrange a marriage, and spending the entire day without finding the person's residence.
  • This clause indicates that the narrator then ordered a servant and a horse to follow the path and go there himself.
  • This clause describes the narrator arriving at a place with sheer cliffs and no village, and finding that the surname in question was very rare in the nearby area.
  • This clause describes the narrator returning home disappointed and forgetting to eat or sleep.
  • This clause describes the narrator developing a confused and disoriented illness, forcing himself to eat soup and porridge, and calling out for '花姑子' in a state of confusion.
  • This clause describes the family not understanding the narrator's condition but watching over him all night, and the narrator being in a critical state.
  • This clause describes the watcher becoming exhausted and falling asleep, and the narrator feeling someone touching and stroking him in a state of drowsiness.
  • This clause describes the narrator slightly opening his eyes and seeing '花姑子' standing at the foot of the bed, and feeling his spirit and energy becoming clear.
  • This clause describes the narrator looking at the girl and shedding tears.
  • This clause describes the girl leaning her head and smiling, asking the narrator how he got there, and then climbing onto the bed, sitting on the narrator's thigh, and pressing his temples with both hands.
  • This clause describes the narrator feeling a strong, penetrating scent of musk.
  • This clause describes the narrator pressing for several moments, suddenly feeling sweat filling his forehead and gradually spreading to his limbs.

Proper Nouns

  • 花姑子 - Nickname or specific person's name, possibly a close friend or family member.

[630]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 小語曰 - A formal way of introducing a character's speech.
  • 室中多人，我不便住 - Literally means 'There are many people in the room, it is inconvenient for me to stay.' Implies a sense of discomfort or awkwardness.
  • 三日當復相望 - Literally means 'In three days, I will see you again.' A polite way of saying goodbye.
  • 又於绣祛中出数蒸饼置床头 - Literally means 'She also took out several steamed buns from her sleeve and placed them on the bed.' A common gesture in classical literature to show generosity or care.
  • 悄然遂去 - Literally means 'She quietly left.' Emphasizes the sudden and silent departure.
  • 安至中夜，汗已思食 - Literally means 'An reached midnight, sweating and thinking of food.' Suggests a state of hunger or distress.
  • 扪饼啖之 - Literally means 'He touched the buns and ate them.' Implies a desperate or urgent action.
  • 不知所苞何料，甘美非常 - Literally means 'He did not know what ingredients were inside, but they were extremely sweet and delicious.' Highlights the unexpected quality of the food.
  • 遂尽三枚 - Literally means 'He finished eating three of them.' Shows the quantity consumed.
  • 又以衣覆余饼 - Literally means 'He then covered the remaining buns with his clothes.' Suggests a protective or caring action.
  • 懵腾酣睡 - Literally means 'He fell into a deep sleep.' Describes a state of deep unconsciousness.
  • 辰分始醒 - Literally means 'He woke up at dawn.' Marks the time of waking.
  • 如释重负 - Literally means 'As if a heavy burden had been lifted.' Describes a feeling of relief.
  • 三日饼尽，精神倍爽 - Literally means 'After three days, the buns were all gone, and his spirit was greatly refreshed.' Shows the positive effect of the food.
  • 乃遣散家人 - Literally means 'He then dismissed his family.' Suggests a decision to be alone.
  • 又虑女来不得其门而入 - Literally means 'He also worried that the woman might not find the door to enter.' Shows concern about the woman's ability to find him.
  • 潜出斋庭，悉脱扃键 - Literally means 'He secretly left the study courtyard and unlocked all the doors and locks.' Describes a stealthy and thorough action.
  • 未几女果至 - Literally means 'Not long after, the woman indeed arrived.' Marks the arrival of the woman.
  • 安喜极，抱与绸缪 - Literally means 'An was extremely happy and embraced her.' Describes a joyful and intimate moment.
  • 恩爱甚至 - Literally means 'Their love was very deep.' Emphasizes the intensity of their affection.
  • 实不能永谐琴瑟，幸早别图 - Literally means 'I cannot truly be a lifelong companion, so I hope you plan for something else soon.' Sets boundaries for their relationship.
  • 安默默良久 - Literally means 'An remained silent for a long time.' Shows his contemplation.
  • 生固求永好 - Literally means 'He firmly requested a permanent relationship.' Shows his persistence.
  • 安闻言，悒悒而悲 - Literally means 'Hearing this, An was deeply sad.' Describes his emotional reaction.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 巫 - Refers to a witch or sorceress, often associated with supernatural powers or rituals.
  • 蒙垢 - Refers to enduring shame or disgrace, often used in the context of taking risks for a greater purpose.
  • 琴瑟 - A metaphor for marital harmony or a harmonious relationship.
  • 夜奔 - Refers to nighttime visits, often with a romantic or illicit connotation.
  • 伉俪 - Refers to a married couple, emphasizing the ideal of a harmonious and devoted partnership.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence introduces a character's speech and her reasons for leaving.
  • This sentence describes a series of actions leading to the woman's departure.
  • This sentence describes An's state of hunger and his actions to satisfy it.
  • This sentence describes the quality and quantity of the food An ate.
  • This sentence describes An's protective action and his subsequent state of deep sleep and relief.
  • This sentence describes the positive effect of the food and An's decision to be alone.
  • This sentence describes An's concern and his actions to facilitate the woman's entry.
  • This sentence describes the woman's arrival and her playful remark.
  • This sentence describes the joyful and intimate moment between An and the woman.
  • This sentence explains the woman's motivations.
  • This sentence sets boundaries for their relationship.
  • This sentence shows An's contemplation and his question about their past connection.
  • This sentence is a cryptic response from the woman.
  • This sentence shows An's persistence in seeking a permanent relationship.
  • This sentence sets clear boundaries for their relationship.
  • This sentence describes An's emotional reaction to the woman's words.
  • This sentence offers a compromise for their relationship.
  • This sentence shows An's attempt to cheer up and his curiosity about the woman's journey.
  • This sentence is a cryptic response from the woman.

[631]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 淹留至今 - Classical expression meaning 'to stay until now,' indicating a prolonged stay.
  • 所疑怪 - Means 'to be doubted or found strange,' referring to potential suspicion or disbelief.
  • 同衾 - Classical expression meaning 'to share a bed,' often used in the context of intimate relationships.
  • 气息肌肤 - Refers to the breath and skin, emphasizing physical closeness and intimacy.
  • 熏何芗泽 - Means 'what fragrance is used,' referring to the scent applied to the body.
  • 由熏饰 - Means 'due to fragrances or adornments,' indicating that the scent is not natural but applied.
  • 草草奔出 - Means 'to rush out in a hurried manner,' describing a quick and possibly embarrassed departure.
  • 且行且詈 - Means 'to walk and scold,' describing a situation where someone is being verbally abused while walking.
  • 潜奔而归 - Means 'to secretly flee back home,' indicating a hasty and hidden return.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 东头聋媪我姨行 - Refers to a deaf aunt on the eastern side of the house, indicating a familial relationship and a specific location.
  • 为君故 - Means 'for your sake,' suggesting a reason for the aunt's prolonged stay.
  • 家中恐所疑怪 - Indicates that the family might suspect or find strange the aunt's prolonged stay, reflecting social norms and expectations.
  • 父母絮絮不寝 - Means 'parents are chattering and not sleeping,' describing a noisy and restless environment.
  • 此宵之会，乃百年之别 - Means 'this night's meeting is a farewell for a lifetime,' indicating a significant and possibly final encounter.
  • 小村孤寂 - Means 'a small and lonely village,' describing the setting and its characteristics.
  • 好合 - Means 'harmonious union,' referring to a favorable marriage or relationship.
  • 尽此夜耳 - Means 'only for this night,' indicating a limited duration of the relationship.
  • 俯仰悲怆 - Means 'to look up and down with sorrow,' describing a state of deep emotional distress.

[632]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 数日徘徊 - Literally 'wandering for several days,' indicating a prolonged state of indecision or hesitation.
  • 心景殆不可过 - Poetic expression meaning 'the mental state is nearly unbearable,' suggesting deep emotional distress.
  • 因思夜往，逾墙以观其便 - Literal translation would be 'thinking to go at night, climbing over the wall to see if it is convenient,' but conveys a sense of stealth and urgency.
  • 叟固言有恩，即令事泄，当无大谴 - Means 'the old man firmly said there is an obligation; even if the matter is revealed, there will be no great punishment,' indicating a promise of leniency.
  • 遂乘夜窜往，蹀躞山中 - Means 'so he secretly went at night, stumbling through the mountains,' conveying a sense of stealth and disorientation.
  • 迷闷不知所往 - Literal translation would be 'bewildered and lost, not knowing where to go,' but conveys a sense of confusion and fear.
  • 大惧 - Straightforward expression of great fear.
  • 方觅归途，见谷中隐有舍宇 - Means 'while searching for the way back, he saw a hidden house in the valley,' indicating a moment of discovery.
  • 重门尚未扃也 - Means 'the heavy doors were not yet locked,' indicating that the house was unoccupied or that the occupants were away.
  • 安向门者讯章氏之居 - Means 'An asked the gatekeeper about the residence of the Zhang family,' indicating a polite inquiry.
  • 有青衣人出，问：“昏夜何人询章氏？” - Means 'a servant dressed in blue came out and asked, 'Who is asking about the Zhang family in the dark night?' indicating a moment of suspicion.
  • 安曰：“是吾亲好，偶迷居向。” - Means 'An said, 'It is a close friend of mine, who is temporarily lost,' indicating a polite explanation.
  • 青衣曰：“男子无问章也。此是渠妗家，花姑即今在此，容传白之。” - Means 'the servant said, 'Men should not ask about the Zhang family. This is the residence of her aunt, and Flower Maiden is here now. Let me inform her,' indicating a cultural taboo and a polite redirection.
  • 入未几，即出邀安 - Means 'after a short time, the servant came out to invite An,' indicating a quick response.
  • 才登廊舍，花姑趋出迎，谓青衣曰：“安郎奔波中夜，想已困殆，可伺床寝。” - Means 'as soon as he entered the hall, Flower Maiden hurried out to greet him, telling the servant, 'An has been traveling all night, he must be tired, let him rest,' indicating a warm welcome and concern.
  • 少间，携手入帏 - Means 'a short while later, she took his hand and led him into the inner chamber,' indicating intimacy.
  • 安问：“妗家何别无人？” - Means 'An asked, 'Why is there no one else in the aunt's house?'' indicating curiosity.
  • 女曰：“妗他出，留妾代守。幸与郎遇，岂非夙缘？” - Means 'the woman said, 'My aunt has gone out, leaving me to guard the house. It is fortunate to meet you, is this not a predestined encounter?'' indicating a sense of fate.
  • 然偎傍之际，觉甚膻腥，心疑有异，女抱安颈，遽以舌舐鼻孔，彻脑如刺。 - Means 'however, as they were close together, he felt a strong odor, suspecting something was amiss. The woman hugged his neck and suddenly licked his nose with her tongue, piercing his brain like a needle,' indicating a shocking and disturbing moment.
  • 安骇绝，急欲逃脱，而身若巨绠之缚，少时闷然不觉矣。 - Means 'An was terrified and tried to escape, but his body was bound as if by a thick rope, and he soon lost consciousness,' indicating a loss of control and consciousness.
  • 安不归，家中逐者穷人迹，或言暮遇于山径者。 - Means 'An did not return home, and those who searched for him found no trace. Some said they saw him on a mountain path in the evening,' indicating a mysterious disappearance.
  • 家人入山，则裸死危崖下。 - Means 'when his family entered the mountain, they found him naked and dead at the foot of a dangerous cliff,' indicating a tragic end.
  • 惊怪莫察其由，舁归。 - Means 'they were shocked and could not understand the cause, so they carried him back home,' indicating a sense of bewilderment and finality.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 叟 - Refers to an old man, often used to denote a wise or experienced person.
  • 章氏 - Refers to the Zhang family, a common surname in Chinese culture.
  • 妗家 - Refers to the residence of the aunt, indicating a familial relationship.
  • 花姑 - Refers to Flower Maiden, a common name for a young woman, often used in literary contexts to denote beauty or grace.
  • 夙缘 - Refers to a predestined encounter or a fateful meeting, a concept rooted in Chinese philosophy and literature.
  • 膻腥 - Refers to a strong, unpleasant odor, often associated with decay or something unnatural.
  • 巨绠之缚 - Refers to being bound as if by a thick rope, indicating a loss of control or a supernatural force.
  • 舁归 - Refers to carrying someone back home, often used in contexts of death or severe injury.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 数日徘徊，心景殆不可过 - Can be read as a continuous state of wandering and emotional distress.
  • 因思夜往，逾墙以观其便 - Can be read as a single action of planning and executing a stealthy visit.
  • 遂乘夜窜往，蹀躞山中 - Can be read as a single action of sneaking into the mountains.
  • 迷闷不知所往 - Can be read as a continuous state of confusion and fear.
  • 方觅归途，见谷中隐有舍宇 - Can be read as a single action of discovering a hidden house.
  • 重门尚未扃也 - Can be read as a single observation about the house being unoccupied.
  • 安向门者讯章氏之居 - Can be read as a single action of inquiring about the residence.
  • 有青衣人出，问：“昏夜何人询章氏？” - Can be read as a single action of the servant questioning An.
  • 安曰：“是吾亲好，偶迷居向。” - Can be read as a single response from An.
  • 青衣曰：“男子无问章也。此是渠妗家，花姑即今在此，容传白之。” - Can be read as a single response from the servant.
  • 入未几，即出邀安 - Can be read as a single action of the servant inviting An.
  • 才登廊舍，花姑趋出迎，谓青衣曰：“安郎奔波中夜，想已困殆，可伺床寝。” - Can be read as a single action of Flower Maiden welcoming An.
  • 少间，携手入帏 - Can be read as a single action of entering the inner chamber.
  • 安问：“妗家何别无人？” - Can be read as a single question from An.
  • 女曰：“妗他出，留妾代守。幸与郎遇，岂非夙缘？” - Can be read as a single response from the woman.
  • 然偎傍之际，觉甚膻腥，心疑有异，女抱安颈，遽以舌舐鼻孔，彻脑如刺。 - Can be read as a single shocking moment of the woman's actions.
  • 安骇绝，急欲逃脱，而身若巨绠之缚，少时闷然不觉矣。 - Can be read as a single action of An's desperate attempt to escape.
  • 安不归，家中逐者穷人迹，或言暮遇于山径者。 - Can be read as a single action of the search and the mysterious sighting.
  • 家人入山，则裸死危崖下。 - Can be read as a single action of finding An's body.
  • 惊怪莫察其由，舁归。 - Can be read as a single action of carrying An back home.

[633]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 眾方聚哭 - A phrase indicating that a group of people is gathered and weeping.
  • 一女郎來吊 - Means 'a young woman came to mourn,' with '吊' specifically indicating the act of mourning.
  • 自门外噭啕而入 - Describes the woman entering loudly and crying, with '噭啕' emphasizing the intensity of her weeping.
  • 抚尸捺鼻，涕洟其中 - A vivid description of the woman touching the body and wiping her nose, with '涕洟' referring to tears and snot.
  • 何愚冥至此 - An exclamatory phrase meaning 'how foolish and blind can it be to this extent,' expressing deep sorrow and lamentation.
  • 痛哭声嘶，移时乃已 - Describes the woman's loud and prolonged crying, with '移时乃已' indicating that it took some time for her to stop.
  • 停以七日，勿殓也 - A directive to delay burial for seven days, with '勿殓' meaning 'do not bury.'
  • 方将启问 - Means 'were about to ask,' indicating the moment when the group was preparing to inquire about the woman.
  • 女傲不为礼 - Describes the woman's arrogant and disrespectful behavior, with '傲' meaning 'arrogant' and '不为礼' meaning 'not showing respect.'

  • 家贫，从副将军贾绾作记室 - '家贫' means 'poor', '从副将军贾绾' means 'followed General Jia Wan', and '作记室' means 'as a secretary'.

  • 有鱼衔龙尾不去，并获之 - '有鱼' means 'a fish', '衔龙尾' means 'bitting the dragon's tail', '不去' means 'would not let go', and '并获之' means 'and caught it together'.
  • 锁置桅间，奄存气息，而龙吻张翕，似求援拯 - '锁置桅间' means 'locked it on the mast', '奄存气息' means 'barely alive', '而龙吻张翕' means 'and the dragon's mouth opened and closed', and '似求援拯' means 'seemed to be asking for help'.
  • 生恻然心动，请于贾而释之 - '生恻然心动' means 'the young man felt compassion', '请于贾' means 'asked General Jia', and '释之' means 'to release it'.
  • 携有金创药，戏敷患处，纵之水中，浮沉逾刻而没 - '携有金创药' means 'carried some medicine for wounds', '戏敷患处' means 'playfully applied it to the wound', '纵之水中' means 'released it into the water', and '浮沉逾刻而没' means 'it floated and sank for a moment before disappearing'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 十年不见 - Reflects the traditional Chinese emphasis on the passage of time and the changes it brings.
  • 穷措大 - A term that highlights the social mobility and aspirations of scholars in Chinese society.
  • 山荆 - A humble term used by men to refer to their wives, reflecting traditional marital roles and modesty.

  • 销魂 - A term often used in classical Chinese poetry and literature to describe a state of being deeply moved or captivated, often by beauty or emotion.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '十年不见，何富贵一至于此' contrasts the passage of time with the dramatic change in the person's status.
  • The rhetorical question '君小觑穷措大不能发迹耶' emphasizes the speaker's rise in social status, challenging the listener's earlier low opinion.
  • The question '携家何往' adds a layer of intrigue to the narrative.
  • The sequence of sensory descriptions '旱雷聒耳，肉竹嘈杂，不复可闻言笑' creates a vivid image of a noisy and chaotic environment.
  • The speaker's offer of a gift, despite the listener's apparent drunkenness, adds a layer of complexity to the interaction.

  • 生人能见之否 - A question about the visibility of the afterlife, reflecting cultural beliefs about the afterlife and the living world.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '一夕，明月莹澈，小步庭中，问女：“冥中亦有城郭否？”' introduces a night scene and a question about the afterlife.
  • The response '答曰：“等耳。冥间城府，不在此处，去此可三四里。但以夜为昼。”' explains the nature of the afterlife and its distance.
  • The question and answer pair '问：“生人能见之否？”答云：“亦可。”' discusses the visibility of the afterlife.
  • The sequence '生请往观，女诺之。乘月去，女飘忽若风，王极力追随，欻至一处，女言：“不远矣。”' describes the journey to the afterlife.
  • The final part '生瞻望殊无所见。女以唾涂其两眦，启之，明倍于常，视夜色不殊白昼。顿见雉堞在杳霭中。路上行人，趋如墟市。' describes the transformation of the protagonist's vision and the sight of the afterlife.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妇思自炫以售，缞绖之中，不忘涂泽 - Highlights the societal pressures on women to maintain their beauty and social status, even in mourning.

  • 母知之，心弗善也，以其将为他人妇，亦隐忍之 - Reflects the traditional role of mothers in Chinese society, often having to endure their children's actions for the sake of family harmony.
  • 于是妇益肆 - Indicates the gradual loss of moral restraint, a common theme in Chinese literature.
  • 村中有无赖子董贵者，见而好之，以金啖金邻妪，求通殷勤于妇 - Portrays the corruption and moral decay in society, with the use of bribery to achieve personal desires.
  • 夜分，由妪家逾墙以达妇所，因与会合 - Describes a clandestine meeting, reflecting the secretive nature of such affairs.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 薄暮 - 薄暮 (bó mù) means 'dusk' or 'late afternoon.'
  • 返取他物，则书已亡 - 返取 (fǎn qǔ) means 'to return and take.' 他物 (tā wù) means 'other things.' 则书已亡 (zé shū yǐ wáng) means 'then the book was gone.'
  • 怪之，仰卧榻上，静息以伺其变 - 怪之 (guài zhī) means 'to find it strange.' 仰卧榻上 (yǎng wò tà shàng) means 'lying on the bed facing up.' 静息以伺其变 (jìng xī yǐ sì qí biàn) means 'to rest quietly and wait for changes.'
  • 食顷 - 食顷 (shí qǐng) means 'a mealtime' or 'about an hour.'
  • 闻步履声，睨之 - 闻步履声 (wén bù lǚ shēng) means 'to hear the sound of footsteps.' 睨之 (nì zhī) means 'to glance at it.'
  • 所亡书送还案上 - 所亡书 (suǒ wáng shū) means 'the lost book.' 送还案上 (sòng huán àn shàng) means 'to return it to the desk.'
  • 逡巡立榻下，相视而笑 - 逡巡 (qūn xún) means 'to hesitate' or 'to linger.' 立榻下 (lì tà xià) means 'standing at the foot of the bed.' 相视而笑 (xiāng shì ér xiào) means 'to look at each other and smile.'
  • 生寂不动 - 生寂不动 (shēng jì bù dòng) means 'the person remains silent and motionless.'
  • 长者翘一足踹生腹 - 长者 (zhǎng zhě) means 'the older one' or 'the elder.' 翘一足 (qiào yī zú) means 'to lift one foot.' 踹生腹 (chuài shēng fù) means 'to kick the person's abdomen.'
  • 少者掩口匿笑 - 少者 (shào zhě) means 'the younger one.' 掩口匿笑 (yǎn kǒu nì xiào) means 'to cover the mouth and secretly laugh.'
  • 生觉心摇摇若不自持 - 生觉心摇摇若不自持 (shēng jué xīn yáo yáo ruò bù zì chí) means 'the person feels his heart shaking as if he cannot control himself.'
  • 即急肃然端念，卒不顾 - 即急肃然端念 (jí jí sù rán duān niàn) means 'immediately becoming serious and concentrating his thoughts.' 卒不顾 (zú bù gù) means 'finally not looking.'
  • 女近以左手捋髭，右手轻批颐颊作小响 - 女近 (nǚ jìn) means 'the woman approaches.' 以左手捋髭 (yǐ zuǒ shǒu lǚ zī) means 'using her left hand to stroke the beard.' 右手轻批颐颊作小响 (yòu shǒu qīng pī yí jiá zuò xiǎo xiǎng) means 'using her right hand to lightly slap the cheeks and make a small sound.'
  • 二女骇奔而散 - 二女骇奔而散 (èr nǚ hài bēn ér sàn) means 'the two women run away in fright.'
  • 生恐夜为所苦，欲移归，又耻其言不掩，乃挑灯读 - 生恐夜为所苦 (shēng kǒng yè wéi suǒ kǔ) means 'the person fears being troubled at night.' 欲移归 (yù yí guī) means 'to want to move back.' 又耻其言不掩 (yòu chǐ qí yán bù yǎn) means 'but is ashamed of his words not being hidden.' 乃挑灯读 (nǎi tiǎo dēng dú) means 'so he picks up a lamp and reads.'
  • 暗中鬼影僮僮，略不顾瞻 - 暗中鬼影僮僮 (àn zhōng guǐ yǐng tóng tóng) means 'in the darkness, ghostly shadows are present.' 略不顾瞻 (lüè bù gù zhān) means 'slightly not looking around.'
  • 夜将半，烛而寝 - 夜将半 (yè jiāng bàn) means 'halfway through the night.' 烛而寝 (zhú ér qǐn) means 'lighting a candle and going to sleep.'
  • 始交睫，觉人以细物穿鼻，奇痒，大嚏 - 始交睫 (shǐ jiāo jié) means 'just as he closed his eyes.' 觉人以细物穿鼻 (jué rén yǐ xì wù chuān bí) means 'feeling someone using a fine object to pierce his nose.' 奇痒 (qí yǎng) means 'extremely itchy.' 大嚏 (dà tì) means 'a big sneeze.'
  • 但闻暗处隐隐作笑声 - 但闻暗处隐隐作笑声 (dàn wén àn chù yǐn yǐn zuò xiào shēng) means 'only hearing faint laughter from the dark.'
  • 生不语，假寐以俟之 - 生不语 (shēng bù yǔ) means 'the person does not speak.' 假寐以俟之 (jiǎ mèi yǐ sì zhī) means 'pretending to sleep and waiting for it.'
  • 俄见少女以纸条拈细股，鹤行鹭伏而至 - 俄见 (é jiàn) means 'soon seeing.' 少女 (shào nǚ) means 'the young girl.' 以纸条拈细股 (yǐ zhǐ tiáo niān xì gǔ) means 'using a paper strip to pick up a fine thread.' 鹤行鹭伏而至 (hè xíng lù fú ér zhì) means 'walking like a crane and crouching like a heron.'
  • 生暴起诃之，飘窜而去 - 生暴起诃之 (shēng bào qǐ hē zhī) means 'the person suddenly stands up and scolds her.' 飘窜而去 (piāo cuàn ér qù) means 'fleeing away.'
  • 既寝，又穿其耳 - 既寝 (jì qǐn) means 'after sleeping.' 又穿其耳 (yòu chuān qí ěr) means 'again piercing his ears.'
  • 终夜不堪其扰 - 终夜不堪其扰 (zhōng yè bù kān qí rǎo) means 'all night long, unable to bear the disturbance.'
  • 鸡既鸣，乃寂无声，生始酣眠，终日无所睹闻 - 鸡既鸣 (jī jì míng) means 'when the rooster crows.' 乃寂无声 (nǎi jì wú shēng) means 'then there is silence.' 生始酣眠 (shēng shǐ hān mián) means 'the person finally sleeps soundly.' 终日无所睹闻 (zhōng rì wú suǒ dǔ wén) means 'throughout the day, nothing is seen or heard.'

  • 林乃托疾，使戚獨宿，遣婢海棠臥其床下。 - This sequence of actions describes Lin's plan.

  • 戚念妻生平從不肯作不速之客，疑而摸其項，無痕，知為婢，又叱之。 - This clause describes Qi's suspicion and actions.

  • 抚育良劬 - Reflects the traditional Chinese emphasis on filial piety and the mother's role in raising children.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 自别生，杜门不交一客 - This phrase can be read as two linked actions: withdrawing from society and isolating oneself.
  • 母诘知故，而志不可夺，亦姑听之 - The sentence structure is elliptical but clear, showing the mother's understanding and reluctant acceptance.
  • 富贾慕细侯名，托媒于媪 - The sentence is concise but conveys the merchant's intention and the mother's involvement.
  • 贾以负贩诣湖南，敬侦生耗 - The sentence is elliptical but clear, showing the merchant's actions and intentions.
  • 狱已将解，贾以金赂当事吏，使久锢之 - The sentence is elliptical but clear, showing the merchant's actions and their consequences.
  • 细侯不得己，遂嫁贾 - The sentence is elliptical but clear, showing Fine Boy's eventual acceptance of the marriage.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 輒摘聖賢頭上旒而煨其板 - Literally 'often plucks the tassels from the heads of sages and roasts them on the boards.' This is a highly metaphorical and likely exaggerated description of the student's behavior.
  • 學官知之，怒欲加刑 - Literally 'the school official knew this and was angry, wanting to punish him.'
  • 馬哀免，願為先生生財 - Literally 'the horse mourned and begged for mercy, willing to make the teacher rich.' This is a metaphorical reference to the student's plea.
  • 學官喜，縱之去 - Literally 'the school official was pleased and let him go.'
  • 馬探其生殷富 - Literally 'the horse probed his life to be wealthy,' meaning the student investigated the teacher's wealth.
  • 登門強索資 - Literally 'went to the door to forcefully demand money.'
  • 故挑其怒 - Literally 'deliberately provoked his anger.'
  • 乃以刀自劙 - Literally 'then cut himself with a knife,' a dramatic act of self-harm.
  • 诬而控诸学 - Literally 'accused him falsely and reported him to the school.'
  • 學官勒取重赂 - Literally 'the school official extorted a large bribe.'
  • 始免申黜 - Literally 'only then was he spared from dismissal.'
  • 諸生因而共愤 - Literally 'the other students thus all became angry.'
  • 公质县尹 - Literally 'the magistrate investigated publicly.'
  • 尹廉得实 - Literally 'the magistrate investigated and found the truth.'
  • 笞四十，梏其颈 - Literally 'whipped forty times and shackled his neck.'
  • 三日毙焉 - Literally 'died after three days.'
  • 是夜，朱叟梦马冠带而入 - Literally 'that night, the old man Zhu dreamed of the horse wearing a hat and entering.'
  • 负公大德，今来相报 - Literally 'repaying a great favor, now comes to repay.'
  • 即寤，妾生子 - Literally 'awoke, and his wife gave birth to a son.'
  • 叟知为马，名以马儿 - Literally 'the old man knew it was the horse, and named the child 'Horse Son'.'
  • 少不慧 - Literally 'was not bright as a child.'
  • 喜其能读 - Literally 'was pleased that he could read.'
  • 二十余，竭力经纪，得入邑庠 - Literally 'in his twenties, worked hard and managed affairs, eventually entered the county school.'
  • 后考试寓旅邸，昼卧床上，见壁间悉糊旧艺 - Literally 'later, during an exam at a lodging, lay on the bed during the day and saw the walls covered with old essays.'
  • 视之有“犬之性”四句题，心畏其难，读而志之 - Literally 'saw the four-line question 'The Nature of Dogs,' was afraid of its difficulty, read it, and memorized it.'
  • 入场，适遇此题，录之，得优等，食饩焉 - Literally 'when entering the examination hall, just encountered this question, wrote it down, and received a top grade, thus receiving a stipend.'
  • 六十余，补临邑训导 - Literally 'in his sixties, was appointed as a teacher in Lin County.'
  • 数年，曾无一道义交 - Literally 'for several years, never had a single moral or ethical relationship.'
  • 惟袖中出青蚨，则作鸬鹚笑；不则睫毛一寸长，棱棱若不相识 - Literally 'only when he took out coins from his sleeve would he laugh like a cormorant; otherwise, his eyebrows would be a foot long, sharp and unrecognizable.'
  • 偶大令以诸生小故，判令薄惩，辄酷烈如治盗贼 - Literally 'once the magistrate punished a student for a minor offense, he would be extremely harsh, treating it like a criminal case.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 今某生貴矣，相狎者猶以為戲 - Colloquial phrase meaning 'Now that someone has become wealthy, those who were once close to him still regard him as a joke.' Conveys a sense of irony or disdain.
  • 山市 - Literally 'mountain market,' but in this context, it refers to a rare atmospheric phenomenon known as a 'mountain city' or 'fairy city.'
  • 邑八景之一也 - Means 'one of the eight scenic spots of the county,' indicating that this phenomenon is a notable local attraction.
  • 數年恒不一見 - Means 'it is rarely seen once every few years,' emphasizing the rarity of the event.
  • 高插青冥 - Means 'towering into the blue sky,' describing the height of the solitary tower.
  • 相顧驚疑 - Means 'looking at each other in surprise and doubt,' capturing the initial reaction of the observers.
  • 念近中無此禅院 - Means 'thinking there is no such temple nearby,' expressing the observers' confusion.
  • 始悟為山市 - Means 'they finally realized it was a mountain city,' indicating the moment of recognition.
  • 高垣睥睨 - Means 'high walls and battlements,' describing the appearance of the city walls.
  • 居然城郭矣 - Means 'it suddenly became a city,' indicating the transformation of the scene.
  • 历历在目 - Means 'clearly visible,' describing the clarity of the view.
  • 尘气莽莽然 - Means 'the dust is thick and hazy,' describing the atmosphere during the wind.
  • 一切乌有 - Means 'everything disappeared,' indicating the sudden vanishing of the scene.
  • 惟危楼一座 - Means 'only a tall building remained,' describing the final structure.
  • 窗扉皆洞开 - Means 'the windows and doors were all open,' indicating the openness of the building.
  • 一行有五点明处 - Means 'there were five bright points in a row,' describing the lights inside the building.
  • 层层指数 - Means 'counting layer by layer,' indicating the process of observing the building's layers.
  • 裁如星点 - Means 'reduced to the size of stars,' describing the shrinking of the building's appearance.
  • 黯然缥缈 - Means 'dim and hazy,' describing the final appearance of the building.
  • 不一状 - Means 'in various forms,' describing the different postures of the people in the building.
  • 倏忽如拳如豆 - Means 'suddenly shrunk to the size of fists and beans,' describing the rapid shrinking of the building.
  • 遂不可见 - Means 'and then it disappeared,' indicating the final vanishing of the building.
  • 见山上人烟市肆 - Means 'seeing people, smoke, and shops on the mountain,' describing the scene of the early traveler.
  • 与世无别 - Means 'no different from the world,' indicating that the scene was similar to the real world.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 山市 - A rare atmospheric phenomenon that has been described in Chinese literature for centuries, often associated with mystical or supernatural events.
  • 邑八景之一也 - Refers to the tradition of identifying and celebrating the most beautiful or notable sights in a region, a common practice in Chinese culture.
  • 高插青冥 - The phrase '青冥' (blue sky) is often used in Chinese literature to describe the vastness and height of the sky, symbolizing the sublime and the divine.
  • 高垣睥睨 - The term '睥睨' (battlements) is a technical term for the parapets on the walls of a city, indicating the defensive nature of the structure.
  • 居然城郭矣 - The term '城郭' (city walls) refers to the fortified walls of a city, a common feature in Chinese urban planning and defense.
  • 尘气莽莽然 - The phrase '莽莽然' (thick and hazy) is often used to describe the atmosphere during natural phenomena, such as dust storms or fog.
  • 一切乌有 - The term '乌有' (disappeared) is a literary device used to describe the sudden vanishing of a scene, often associated with magical or supernatural events.
  • 惟危楼一座 - The term '危楼' (tall building) is often used in Chinese literature to describe structures that are both impressive and awe-inspiring.
  • 窗扉皆洞开 - The term '洞开' (open) indicates the openness and transparency of the building, suggesting a sense of freedom or exposure.
  • 一行有五点明处 - The term '明处' (bright points) is often used to describe lights or sources of illumination, indicating the presence of activity or life within the building.
  • 层层指数 - The term '指数' (counting) is a technical term used to describe the process of measuring or quantifying something, in this case, the layers of the building.
  • 裁如星点 - The term '星点' (star points) is a metaphorical expression used to describe small, distant objects, often associated with the night sky.
  • 黯然缥缈 - The term '缥缈' (dim and hazy) is often used to describe the appearance of distant or fading objects, suggesting a sense of mystery or unreality.
  • 不一状 - The term '不一状' (in various forms) is a descriptive phrase used to indicate diversity or variety, in this case, the different postures of the people in the building.
  • 倏忽如拳如豆 - The term '倏忽' (suddenly) is a temporal adverb used to describe rapid changes, often associated with the fleeting nature of phenomena.
  • 遂不可见 - The term '遂不可见' (and then it disappeared) is a literary device used to describe the final vanishing of a scene, often associated with magical or supernatural events.
  • 见山上人烟市肆 - The term '人烟市肆' (people, smoke, and shops) is a descriptive phrase used to indicate the presence of human activity and commerce, suggesting a bustling and lively scene.
  • 与世无别 - The term '无别' (no different) is a comparative phrase used to indicate similarity or equivalence, in this case, the similarity between the mountain scene and the real world.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '今某生贵矣，相狎者犹以为戏' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'Now that someone has become wealthy, those who were once close to him still regard him as a joke.'
  • The sentence '山市，邑八景之一也，数年恒不一见' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'The mountain city, one of the eight scenic spots of the county, is rarely seen once every few years.'
  • The sentence '孙公子禹年，与同人饮楼上，忽见山头有孤塔耸起，高插青冥' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'Mr. Sun, a gentleman named Yu, was drinking with his companions on the upper floor of a building when he suddenly saw a solitary tower rise from the mountain, towering into the blue sky.'
  • The sentence '相顾惊疑，念近中无此禅院' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'They looked at each other in surprise and doubt, thinking there is no such temple nearby.'
  • The sentence '始悟为山市' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'They finally realized it was a mountain city.'
  • The sentence '未几高垣睥睨，连亘六七里，居然城郭矣' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'Soon after, high walls and battlements appeared, stretching for six or seven miles, and it suddenly became a city.'
  • The sentence '中有楼若者、堂若者、坊若者，历历在目，以亿万计' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'Inside, there were buildings like towers, halls, and marketplaces, clearly visible, numbering in the millions.'
  • The sentence '忽大风起，尘气莽莽然，城市依稀而已' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'Suddenly, a strong wind arose, and the dust became thick and hazy, and the city became faintly visible.'
  • The sentence '既而风定天清，一切乌有；惟危楼一座，直接霄汉' is elliptical and grammatically compact. A more complete version would be: 'After the wind subsided and the sky cleared, everything disappeared; only a tall building remained, reaching directly into the heavens.'
